_meta.description: Hand-labeled ground truth for the 30 synthetic emails. Used for measuring classification accuracy and calibrating prioritization. Labeled by hand BEFORE running the system on the dataset.
labeling_principles: Categories are not always clean — when ambiguity exists, the label reflects how Ryan would actually want it handled, not strict category boundaries.
needs_ryan is true only when Ryan personally must be the responder. Important + delegable = needs_ryan false.
Priority is on a 0-100 scale matching the prioritizer rubric in prioritizer.py.

labels.notes: Hot inbound from $180M plant-based brand with specific need, referral, and multi-stakeholder intent. Ryan's bread and butter.
Small co-founder ($8M rev) below ICP. Polite delegation to BDR for qualification.
Formal RFP from large multi-brand operator with hard deadline. High-value strategic prospect.
Stripe payout notification. Pure transactional.
Real data quality issue + churn risk language ('threatening to revert to manual') + audit deadline. Top customer signal.
Generic outbound with unfilled template variables ({{company_name_1}}). Spam pattern.
Seat addition + expansion interest. Important but CS/admin can handle the seat add; expansion question may loop in Ryan later.
Strong applicant (130% quota, relevant CPG domain). Recruiter screens first, surface to Ryan if it passes initial bar.
LinkedIn notification digest.
Seat reduction + pricing negotiation request ahead of renewal. Ryan must own this conversation.
Former CircleUp colleague + adjacent startup + raising + warm intro from USV partner. Mixes personal/professional/competitive. Ryan must respond personally.
Mid-stage deal with pricing objection and tight timeline. BDR/AE owns, but worth Ryan's awareness for the pricing flexibility decision.
Calendly notification — meeting is already on calendar.
Strong recruiter pitch with directly-relevant candidate. Recruiter qualifies, surface to Ryan if first call goes well.
Production outage for an active customer with 90-min deadline. Engineering on it immediately; Ryan needs to be aware/respond personally given customer profile.
Product Hunt daily digest.
VC intro when not raising. Not sales_inquiry (won't buy), but worth a personal short response to maintain relationship for later.
Customer frustration escalating ('renewed contract partly on this expectation'). Churn risk signal embedded in support ticket. Ryan needs to engage personally even if response is mostly drafted.
SaaStr event marketing blast.
$300M frozen brand COO references podcast, asks for personal call. Warm, high-intent, high-fit.
Gong follow-up. Not spam (real product Ryan might use eventually) but not a priority. Archive or delegate to whoever owns sales tech stack.
Routine onboarding question from new customer. CS owns.
GitHub notification. Tracked in eng tools — doesn't need email triage attention.
Customer thank-you with positive signal (new contract closed). Short warm response from Ryan strengthens relationship. Not urgent but high-value relational touch.
Outbound from a list-building service. Not spam exactly but not relevant.
Active cancellation due to acquisition. Top priority — Ryan needs to engage on testimonial, references, and potentially explore enterprise relationship with Sun-Maid.
Cold inbound, minimal info, but right ICP. BDR qualifies.
PE firm offering portfolio company intro AND ongoing portfolio relationship. High strategic value, Ryan must own.
Routine product feedback from happy customer. CS captures, product reviews. Nice signal but not urgent.
Outbound 'replace your BDRs' pitch. Off-base — Waystation is explicitly building a human BDR team.
